statement.markdown: Write a function that takes a 2D array as input and returns a new array with all sub-arrays flattened. The function should handle nested arrays of arbitrary depth. However, the current implementation has a logical bug and does not produce the expected output.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a 2D array of integers, strings, or other arrays.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a new array with all sub-arrays flattened.
statement.notes: The function should be efficient and handle large inputs.

constraints.description: Maximum number of elements in the input array

examples.explanation: The function should flatten the input array
The function should handle nested arrays of arbitrary depth

hints.text: Use recursion to handle nested arrays

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to use recursion to handle nested arrays of arbitrary depth
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to use a loop to iterate over each element in the array and recursively call the function on each sub-array
editorial.betterSolution: However, this approach would be inefficient and may cause stack overflow errors for large inputs
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a recursive function with a base case to handle the end of the array and a recursive case to handle each sub-array
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by induction
commonMistakes: Not handling the base case correctly
Not using recursion to handle nested arrays
